summary.brief: Herrick v. Zaghlool. Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: This case stems from Herrick’s allegation that Dr. Zaghlool improperly performed an excision of a lymph node on June 13, 2017, causing pain and other symptoms to his left arm and shoulder.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Herrick had two post-operative appointments with Dr. Zaghlool—June 19 and 26, 2017—during which Herrick relayed to Dr. Zaghlool his worsening symptoms.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Herrick pursued an appointment with his primary care provider and was seen by Charlie Davis, PA-C (“Davis”) in his primary-care-provider’s office on July 28, 2017.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Randall saw Herrick on August 7 and 28, and September 6, 2017. Based on his examination of Herrick, Dr. Randall ordered a MRI, a MRI (with contrast), and an EMG.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Herrick consulted Steven Shook, M.D. (“Dr. Shook”) of the Cleveland Clinic on November 16, 2017, who told Herrick that he sustained an injury to his spinal accessory nerve as a result of the surgery performed by Dr. Zaghlool.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Therefore, because Herrick failed to timely file his suit against defendants, summary judgment in favor of defendants was appropriate.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Summary judgment is proper where there is no genuine issue of material fact, the moving party is entitled to judgment as a matter of law, and reasonable minds can reach but one conclusion when viewing the evidence in favor of the non-moving party, and the conclusion is adverse to the non-moving party.
evidence_index.speaker: Court reasoning

initial_state.chief_complaint: Pain and other symptoms in left arm and shoulder
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient underwent excision of a lymph node on June 13, 2017, and experienced worsening symptoms post-operatively.

focused_exam.musculoskeletal: Collapsing trapezius muscle, scapula winging, unable to raise arm

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Post-operative complications following lymph node excision

requestables.name: MRI with contrast
requestables.description: MRI of the shoulder with contrast

timeline_phases.name: Further Evaluation
timeline_phases.description: Patient seeks further evaluation due to worsening symptoms

key_events.event: Consultation with primary care provider and referral to orthopedic surgeon
Consultation with Dr. Randall and diagnostic tests ordered

decision_points.prompt: What should have been done after the patient reported worsening symptoms?

options.description: Re-evaluate the patient and consider further diagnostic testing
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard practice to investigate unexplained post-operative symptoms

options.description: Advise patient to wait for symptoms to resolve over time
options.clinical_reasoning: Patient's symptoms were not improving, indicating further evaluation was necessary

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant advised patient to wait for symptoms to resolve
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant stated it would take a month or two to get back to normal

expected_action_court.description: Re-evaluate the patient and consider further diagnostic testing
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence suggested further investigation was warranted

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Based on evidence E005, further investigation was necessary
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Patient should have been re-evaluated and further tests ordered
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient diagnosed with scapula winging, torn labrum, and brachial-plexus injury

key_findings: Summary judgment granted due to statute of limitations
No genuine issue of material fact regarding timely filing

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Timely filing could have allowed for case evaluation

factual_timeline.event: Excision of lymph node performed by Dr. Zaghlool
Consultation with primary care provider and referral to orthopedic surgeon
Consultation with Dr. Randall and diagnostic tests ordered
Consultation with Dr. Shook and diagnosis of spinal accessory nerve injury

treatments.treatment: Arm sling and referral to pain management

complications.complication: Scapula winging, torn labrum, brachial-plexus injury

allegations.allegation: Improper performance of lymph node excision

court_findings.finding: Summary judgment granted due to statute of limitations
court_findings.legal_significance: No genuine issue of material fact regarding timely filing
